message:
fix(sync/rwlock): wake blocked readers when last queued writer is dropped

When a queued (pending) writer future is dropped from both WriteFuture
and OwnedWriteFuture, the code now checks whether that was the last
writer in the queue. If writer_waiters reaches 0 and no writer is
currently active, all blocked reader waiters are drained and woken so
they can proceed concurrently. Without this fix, readers could remain
blocked indefinitely after all contending writers had been cancelled or
dropped — a subtle starvation scenario that only manifests under
cancellation of write-lock acquisitions while readers are queued behind
them.

Includes a new unit test (test_drop_queued_writer_wakes_readers_when_readers_active)
that reproduces the exact scenario: hold a read lock, queue a writer,
queue a second reader behind the writer, drop the writer, and assert the
second reader is woken.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
